commit_msg: docs: add CRUD operations guide to README
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Confession in Plain Text
subtitle: He wrote down everything. Left it on the table. Called it a guide.
when_where: 🕐 9:42 AM, Thursday morning. Main branch, the kind of daylight when honest work gets done. 📋 The README sat open like a confessional booth, waiting for him to speak. He knew what he was about to do—document the whole operation in detail, line by line, for anyone with eyes to read.
who_whom: 🔍 Omkar faced the blank page like an interrogator facing himself. The app's CRUD operations—Create, Read, Update, Delete—were the four walls of his cell. 💻 He wasn't hiding anything anymore. He was *explaining* it. Making it beautiful. Making it teachable. Making it *inevitable*.
what_why: ✍️ He typed it all out: how to create, how to retrieve, how to modify, how to destroy. 📖 Every sentence was a footprint leading directly to him. He told himself it was *helping others*—that documentation was *noble*—but the real crime was the clarity. 🧠 By explaining the operation so perfectly, he made it reproducible. By making it reproducible, he made himself disposable. 💀 The guide was his masterpiece and his epitaph.
verdict: He documented the crime so well, he made himself obsolete.
